name: Match PKS Design to Natural Parts
description: Takes a PKS design from RetroTide or TridentSynth and automatically finds matching natural biological parts in ClusterCAD's database of 531 real PKS clusters. For each module in the design, searches for natural modules with matching domain profiles (AT substrate, reductive loop domains) and returns the amino acid sequences for all domains in each match. Only call this tool when the user explicitly asks for amino acid sequences or natural parts for a design.

inputs.name: design
inputs.description: A single PKS design object. For RetroTide: a dict with 'modules' (list of {loading, domains}). For TridentSynth: a dict containing 'pks_modules' or the full result dict.

inputs.name: source
inputs.description: Which tool produced the design: 'retrotide' or 'tridentsynth'.

inputs.name: max_matches_per_module
inputs.description: Maximum number of ClusterCAD matches to return per module. Default 3.

outputs.description: Structured result with: source (str), total_modules (int), modules_with_matches (int), module_matches (array of per-module results each containing module_index, loading, design_domains, and natural_matches with accession, cluster_name, subunit_name, subunit_id, module_label, and domains array with domain_type, domain_id, annotation, and aa_sequence), and warnings (array of strings).

examples.notes: RetroTide design with 2 modules — returns matching natural parts with AA sequences from ClusterCAD.

trigger_phrases: find natural parts for this design
get amino acid sequences for this PKS
find real parts for retrotide design
what natural modules match this design
get AA sequences for these modules
find biological parts for this PKS design
